text: I feel intrigued and slightly awed by my surroundings. The first space is minimalist and almost clinical in its emptiness, inviting contemplation and a sense of anticipation. The second space bursts into color and information—walls speak of research, development, and the intimacy of human curiosity. I am reminded how even the most impersonal settings can become stages for discovery. I feel as though I’m poised at the intersection of thought and experience, drawn into a narrative of inquiry that asks me to wonder, to reflect, and perhaps to become a little more curious myself.
